title: Quantitative Real‐Time Polymerase Chain Reaction (qRT‐PCR)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2q796","text":"This protocol explains Quantitative Real‐Time Polymerase Chain Reaction (qRT‐PCR) of lines ND1014, N1, and ND27760 from Synthetic mRNAs Drive Highly Efficient iPS Cell Differentiation to Dopaminergic Neurons. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":120,"length":87}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"d4fo0","text":"Obtain approval to work with human stem cells from an appropriate Institutional Review Board. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"3926s","text":"Please refer to the Safety Data Sheets (SDS) for safety and environmental hazards. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Extract total RNA using a RNeasy Mini Kit.
Reverse transcribe using murine leukemia virus reverse transcriptase and oligo(dT) primers.
Set up qRT-PCR using SYBR green PCR Master Mix in the IQ5 RT‐PCR detection system. Primer sequences are listed in "Guidelines".
creator: Anita Broellochs
